focus: traceability - README cluster: serverInfo 1.8.0, TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length, SPECKIT_BACKEND_ONLY, error codes, schema v28-v30

findingDetails.title: README proxy replay boundary is mislabeled as read-only
findingDetails.claim: The README cluster tells operators that the front-proxy transparently replays read-only tools across a recycle.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked the two README surfaces in the assigned cluster and the proxy allow-list/classifier; no README caveat names memory_save or says the replay boundary includes non-read mutating calls.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: memory_save may be intended to be safe or idempotent enough for replay, and the source excludes more destructive tools via UNSAFE_TOOL_NAMES. That still does not make it read-only, and the source comment calls secondary-index idempotency a known follow-up.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if maintainers define memory_save replay as read-only-equivalent in operator docs, or if source removes memory_save from REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES so the README boundary becomes literally true.
findingDetails.scopeProof: Exact search plus direct reads found read-only replay wording in both README surfaces and non-read replayable memory_save in shared proxy source.
findingDetails.recommendation: Replace read-only with replayable/idempotent wording and explicitly mention memory_save, or remove memory_save from REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES if the intended contract is truly read-only replay.

traceabilityChecks.serverInfo: source 1.8.0 confirmed; no new README-cluster 1.7.2 hit; prior spec-doc drift already recorded as R3-P1-001
traceabilityChecks.toolCount: README cluster preserves 36; TOOL_DEFINITIONS list has 36 entries; prior non-README count drift already recorded as R3-P1-002
traceabilityChecks.backendOnly: README and ENV rows align with context-server backendOnly guard
traceabilityChecks.errorCodes: README error-code table aligns with launcher -32001/-32002 definitions and terminal CLOSED behavior
traceabilityChecks.schemaVersions: README schema v28-v30 narrative aligns with SCHEMA_VERSION 30 and migrations 28,29,30
traceabilityChecks.replayBoundary: new P1 finding: README read-only replay wording conflicts with memory_save in REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Assigned slice spans three README-cluster docs plus source-of-truth files for tool registry, server metadata, env guard, schema migrations, and launcher proxy error/replay semantics.

targetSelection.selectionReason: README cluster claims were checked against deployed source anchors named by the prompt: serverInfo, tool count, backend-only env, launcher errors/replay, and schema v28-v30.

searchLedger.hypothesis: README replay-boundary wording underreports replayable mutations.

searchActions.result: Both README surfaces label transparent replay as read-only.
REPLAYABLE_TOOL_NAMES includes memory_save and classifyFrame marks allow-listed tools replayable; memory_save idempotency is explicitly a known follow-up.

searchLedger.rationale: The docs' read-only replay boundary is false for memory_save, a mutating tool in the replay allow-list.

title: README proxy replay boundary is mislabeled as read-only

detail: Classified R10-P1-001 as P1 because the operator-facing README cluster states a read-only replay boundary while source replays memory_save and marks its idempotency as a known follow-up.
serverInfo source is 1.8.0; no new README-cluster 1.7.2 citation found beyond already-recorded R3-P1-001.
README cluster preserves 36 and TOOL_DEFINITIONS lists 36 registrations.
README and ENV_REFERENCE SPECKIT_BACKEND_ONLY rows align with context-server backendOnly guard.
README schema v28-v30 and error-code narratives match source constants and migrations.

README-cluster count surfaces use 36, matching TOOL_DEFINITIONS.length from the 36-entry registry list
SPECKIT_BACKEND_ONLY docs match the source stdio skip guard
-32001 and -32002 README meanings match launcher constants and terminal CLOSED behavior
